RULING: Defendant O'Reilly Auto Enterprises, LLC F/K/A CSK Auto, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Adjudication

Counsel Mia Mattis of Gold Law Firm waives right to oral argument. Counsel H.W. Trey Jones to argue for plaintiff, and counsel is to file a Notice of Association by Friday June 19, 2020 end of business.

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant O'Reilly Auto Enterprises, LLC F/K/A CSK Auto, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to false representation and punitive damages. Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855. Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses restate their allegations and simply provide laundry lists of people and/or documents. (Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal. App. 4th 96, 107.) As to Plaintiffs' claim for false representation, Plaintiffs fail to provide specific evidence showing that a misrepresentation was made. As to Plaintiffs' cause of action for punitive damages, Defendant has demonstrated that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

RULING: DEFENDANT GENUINE PARTS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Genuine Parts Company's Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to false representation and DENIED as to punitive damages. As to Plaintiffs' claim for false representation, Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiffs fail to provide specific evidence showing that a misrepresentation was made. As to Plaintiffs' cause of action for punitive damages, Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (see Azad Declaration, Ex. H [letter from Ted Bennett, Vice President of the Rayloc Division of GPC to Larry Adams of Abex Corporation, dated November 1, 1984].) Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant J.T. Thorpe and Son Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED.

Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826 by demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence that Mr. Van Tassell was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. Plaintiffs concede in their interrogatory responses that they "do not currently allege Plaintiff Fred Van Tassell suffered any asbestos exposures while serving in the Navy between the years 1960 to 1980." (Hoye Declaration, Ex. D at 4:1-9.) Mr. Van Tassell also testified in his deposition that he never saw anyone perform any repair work on boilers. (Hoye Declaration, Ex. G at 224.)

Plaintiffs failed to present any evidence creating any triable issue of material fact. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
